safe_actions.title: Publish next content plan brief
safe_actions.description: No site changes detected. Growth mode: publish next queued blog post from content_plan_briefs.json. Prioritise alternative/comparison cluster (highest conversion probability: 0.91).

safe_actions.title: Internal linking audit — reinforce pillar pages
safe_actions.description: Growth mode: run linking cluster map from serp_strategy.py. Ensure every spoke article links to its pillar page. Add missing anchor text links from blog posts to /gtm-audit.

safe_actions.title: Improve low-CTR pages from GSC
safe_actions.description: Growth mode: pull pages with >100 impressions and <3% CTR from GSC. Rewrite meta descriptions to match the top triggering query per page.

safe_actions.title: Resubmit sitemap to GSC
safe_actions.description: Growth mode: ping GSC sitemap endpoint to trigger a fresh crawl. Ensures any recent content is picked up in the next index cycle.

safe_actions.title: Add new glossary term from keyword gaps
safe_actions.description: Growth mode: pick the top-traffic, low-difficulty keyword from SERP_KEYWORD_CLUSTERS that lacks a dedicated glossary page. Create it.

safe_actions.title: Title too short (8 chars)
safe_actions.description: AI-generated or auto-populated titles under 30 characters are unlikely to be descriptive enough for search snippets.
safe_actions.fix_instructions: Expand the title to 50-65 characters with the primary keyword and brand name (e.g., 'Keyword | Pipeleap').

safe_actions.title: Missing H1
safe_actions.description: The page does not expose a primary H1 heading.
safe_actions.fix_instructions: Add one H1 that reflects the primary keyword and user intent.

safe_actions.title: No structured data (JSON-LD) detected
safe_actions.description: The page has no JSON-LD schema markup. Structured data is required for rich results (FAQ, HowTo, Article, Product) and helps Google understand page context for AI Overviews.
safe_actions.fix_instructions: Add a relevant JSON-LD block in the <head>. For blog posts use Article or BlogPosting; for product/landing pages use SoftwareApplication or Product; for FAQ sections use FAQPage.

safe_actions.title: Meta description too short (48 chars)
safe_actions.description: Short meta descriptions (under 70 characters) waste the snippet space and reduce click-through rates.
safe_actions.fix_instructions: Expand to 140-160 characters with a keyword, value proposition, and CTA.

safe_actions.title: No WebSite structured data detected
safe_actions.description: No WebSite JSON-LD schema was found across crawled pages. Google uses WebSite structured data (with name and url properties) to determine the preferred site name shown in search results. Without it, Google may display an inaccurate or sub-optimal site name.

keyword_opportunities.keyword: how to build outbound sales system for B2B SaaS
outbound sales automation for small sales teams
what is managed outbound sales service
how long does GTM implementation take

content_generated.title: Apollo.io vs Clay vs Pipeleap: What Is the Difference?
structure: H1: Apollo.io vs Clay vs Pipeleap: What Is the Difference?
Problem frame: What most teams get wrong about Apollo.io alternatives
Implication: What happens if this stays broken (pipeline, quota, headcount)
Solution reveal: How the right system changes the outcome
content_generated.persona: Head of Sales evaluating outbound tools
eeat_notes: Include decision framework: who should pick which tool
Add outcome proof for Pipeleap managed service

content_generated.title: Outbound Sales Automation
content_generated.definition: Outbound sales automation is the use of software and AI to execute prospecting, lead enrichment, personalised outreach, and follow-up sequences without manual rep effort. An automated outbound system runs continuously, learns from reply signals, and routes qualified prospects into the CRM pipeline.

content_generated.title: Sales Orchestration
content_generated.definition: Sales orchestration is the coordination of every component in an outbound sales system — lead sourcing, enrichment, personalisation, sequencing, reply handling, and CRM handoff — into a single governed workflow. Unlike point tools that automate one slice, an orchestrated system manages the entire motion as one continuous pipeline.

content_generated.title: GTM Audit
content_generated.definition: A GTM (go-to-market) audit is a structured review of a company's outbound sales motion, covering ICP definition, lead targeting, messaging quality, outreach workflow, and pipeline health. The output is a prioritised list of gaps and fixes that improve pipeline predictability.

pages_optimized.recommended_title: Free GTM Audit for B2B Sales Teams | Pipeleap
pages_optimized.recommended_meta: Get a full audit of your outbound engine - targeting, messaging, workflows, and pipeline health. Identify what is broken and get a fix plan in under 2 weeks.

competitor_insights.their_strength: Clay is a data enrichment and workflow building platform that connects to multiple enrichment sources via a waterfall model.
pipeleap_wins: Unified enrichment + sequencing + CRM routing in one workflow engine
Signal-based triggering without manual recipe building
Governed CRM write-back on every workflow trigger
Predictable pricing that scales with outbound volume, not credits
limitations: No native outbound sequencing — requires Smartlead or Instantly integration
Waterfall enrichment requires manual recipe configuration and ongoing maintenance
No CRM write-back governance layer — data routing is manual
Pricing scales sharply with enrichment credits at volume
Primarily a data tool — outbound execution requires additional tools
competitor_insights.content_to_outrank: Publish 'Clay vs Pipeleap' comparison page targeting transactional intent. Lead with concrete outcome differentials.
competitor_insights.best_for: Teams that need waterfall enrichment but already have sequencing and CRM tools configured

competitor_insights.their_strength: Zapier is a general-purpose automation platform that connects apps via triggers and actions.
pipeleap_wins: Purpose-built for outbound workflow orchestration — not generic automation
Native outbound logic: signal detection, enrichment, sequencing, routing
Governed execution with audit trails and workflow performance tracking
Designed for revenue teams, not IT departments
limitations: General-purpose automation — not built for revenue workflow execution
No native understanding of sales sequences, CRM routing, or outbound logic
Brittle multi-step workflows break under high volume
No governance layer for outbound execution
Pricing escalates sharply with task volume
competitor_insights.content_to_outrank: Publish 'Zapier vs Pipeleap' comparison page targeting transactional intent. Lead with concrete outcome differentials.
competitor_insights.best_for: Simple one-step automations between apps that don't require revenue workflow logic
